primary_site.evidence: punch biopsy followed by wide excisional biopsy of the right scalp lesion revealed nodular melanoma

cancer_category.evidence: right scalp lesion revealed nodular melanoma with Breslow thickness of 6.2 mm

overall_stage_at_diagnosis.evidence: the patient was classified as Stage IV M1d due to brain metastases

t_stage_at_diagnosis.evidence: Breslow thickness of 6.2 mm, Clark level V, ulceration present -- AJCC T4b (>4.0 mm with ulceration)

n_stage_at_diagnosis.evidence: palpable right cervical lymph node fine needle aspiration confirmed metastatic melanoma; bulky right cervical lymphadenopathy measuring 4.1 cm

m_stage_at_diagnosis.evidence: the patient was classified as Stage IV M1d due to brain metastases

histology.evidence: right scalp lesion revealed nodular melanoma with Breslow thickness of 6.2 mm, Clark level V

diagnosis_date.evidence: At age 67, punch biopsy followed by wide excisional biopsy of the right scalp lesion revealed nodular melanoma. No specific calendar date provided in notes.

biomarker_BRAF_V600E.evidence: comprehensive genomic profiling of the primary tumor identified BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 55%)

biomarker_TMB.evidence: high tumor mutational burden of 28 mutations per megabase

biomarker_LDH_baseline.evidence: serum LDH was significantly elevated at 485 U/L

biomarker_HMB45.evidence: metastatic melanoma, positive for HMB-45 and SOX10

biomarker_SOX10.evidence: metastatic melanoma, positive for HMB-45 and SOX10

regimen_1_drug_list.evidence: colitis resolved to grade 1 over 3 weeks with steroid taper, and the decision was made to continue nivolumab monotherapy. Colitis is classic ipilimumab+nivolumab toxicity; switch to nivolumab monotherapy implies prior combination.

regimen_1_start_date.evidence: At age 67, colitis resolved... decision was made to continue nivolumab monotherapy. Combination therapy preceded this, starting at age 67.

regimen_1_toxicity_list.evidence: colitis resolved to grade 1 over 3 weeks with steroid taper

regimen_2_drug_list.evidence: the decision was made to continue nivolumab monotherapy

regimen_2_start_date.evidence: At age 67, colitis resolved to grade 1 over 3 weeks with steroid taper, and the decision was made to continue nivolumab monotherapy

regimen_3_drug_list.evidence: decision was made to switch to BRAF/MEK inhibitor combination therapy; restaging CT at 8 weeks on dabrafenib/trametinib showed significant response

regimen_3_start_date.evidence: At age 68, given hepatic progression on nivolumab monotherapy with confirmed BRAF V600E mutation, decision was made to switch to BRAF/MEK inhibitor combination therapy

surgery_1_type.evidence: punch biopsy followed by wide excisional biopsy of the right scalp lesion

surgery_1_site.evidence: wide excisional biopsy of the right scalp lesion

surgery_1_date.evidence: At age 67, punch biopsy followed by wide excisional biopsy of the right scalp lesion

radiation_1_type.evidence: MRI brain at 12 weeks post-SRS showed treatment effect with decreased enhancement of both lesions

radiation_1_site.value: brain (right frontal lobe, left parietal lobe)
radiation_1_site.evidence: two enhancing lesions in the right frontal lobe (1.8 cm) and left parietal lobe (1.2 cm) consistent with brain metastases; MRI brain at 12 weeks post-SRS showed treatment effect with decreased enhancement of both lesions

radiation_1_start_date.evidence: At age 68, MRI brain at 12 weeks post-SRS showed treatment effect. SRS would have been performed approximately 12 weeks prior, at age 67.

burden_1_assessor_type.evidence: RADIOLOGY REPORT FINDINGS: At age 67, restaging CT at 12 weeks showed mixed response

burden_1_type.evidence: restaging CT at 12 weeks showed mixed response with reduction in pulmonary nodules but stable hepatic lesions, assessed as stable disease per RECIST 1.1

burden_1_detail.evidence: assessed as stable disease per RECIST 1.1

burden_2_assessor_type.evidence: RADIOLOGY REPORT FINDINGS: At age 68, MRI brain at 12 weeks post-SRS showed treatment effect

burden_2_type.evidence: MRI brain at 12 weeks post-SRS showed treatment effect with decreased enhancement of both lesions and no new intracranial lesions

burden_2_detail.value: treatment effect with decreased enhancement of both brain lesions, no new intracranial lesions
burden_2_detail.evidence: MRI brain at 12 weeks post-SRS showed treatment effect with decreased enhancement of both lesions and no new intracranial lesions

burden_3_type.evidence: LDH trended down to 342 U/L suggesting partial biochemical response to therapy

burden_3_detail.value: partial biochemical response, LDH decreased from 485 to 342 U/L
burden_3_detail.evidence: LDH trended down to 342 U/L suggesting partial biochemical response to therapy

burden_4_type.evidence: restaging PET/CT at 6 months showed continued partial response in pulmonary metastases and stable hepatic disease with decreased FDG uptake

burden_4_detail.value: partial response in pulmonary metastases, stable hepatic disease with decreased FDG uptake
burden_4_detail.evidence: restaging PET/CT at 6 months showed continued partial response in pulmonary metastases and stable hepatic disease with decreased FDG uptake

burden_5_detail.value: progression of hepatic metastases with new lesion in left hepatic lobe, stable pulmonary disease
burden_5_detail.evidence: restaging CT at 9 months showed progression of hepatic metastases with new lesion in left hepatic lobe despite stable pulmonary disease

burden_7_detail.evidence: restaging CT at 8 weeks on dabrafenib/trametinib showed significant response with greater than 50% reduction in hepatic metastases and continued stability of pulmonary disease
